summary: Decompiled a large additional slice of Donkey Kong special-move code, primarily SpecialN/Giant Punch state callbacks and transitions, while adding an initial ftdonkey6.c for SpecialHi start actions. The PR removed most of the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdonkey_2.s, added ftdonkey6.c to obj_files.mk, expanded Donkey attributes and generic Fighter stateVar unions, and renamed several formerly generic func_/lbl_ symbols to ftDonkey_ names in action-state tables. There were no review comments in the provided slice.

key_files.reason: Large assembly deletion and table-symbol updates; most of the decompiled functions were removed from this assembly unit, while remaining action-state table entries were renamed from func_/lbl_ to ftDonkey_ symbols.

Main C implementation added for Donkey SpecialN/Giant Punch behavior, including action transitions, callbacks, charge state handling, effect spawning, ground velocity update, ground/air transitions, and effect-destroy helpers.
New C file added for Donkey SpecialHi start actions and callback setup for grounded and aerial variants.
Expanded ftDonkeyAttributes layout, corrected x30 from f32 to s32, added x48/x4C/x50/x54 fields, adjusted filler size, and declared ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffects.
Extended generic Fighter stateVar unions with signed and float aliases used by the new Donkey code, notably x234C_stateVar4_s32/f32 and x2354_stateVar6_s32.
Updated an external prototype/comment from func_8010FAF0 to ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffectsPlus.
Changed ftDonkey_8010D774 to call the newly named ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffectsPlus helper.
Added ftdonkey6.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES so the new C translation unit participates in the build.

what_changed: Moved many Donkey special functions from assembly into C, reducing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdonkey_2.s by about 1566 deleted lines and adding about 426 lines to ftdonkey5.c.

Implemented helper callback setup for SpecialN as ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialN, assigning OnTakeDamage/OnDeath2 to ftDonkey_8010D774, effect cleanup to ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffects, and hitlag callbacks to efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll.
Added ftDonkey_NullCallbacks to clear SpecialN-related effect and hitlag callbacks before returning to common idle/fall transitions.

Replaced local float label extern use in ftdonkey5.c with literal 0.0f and 1.0f for many Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls, enabling removal of lbl_804D95D8/lbl_804D95E0/lbl_804D95E4 constants from the assembly file.

Implemented SpecialN charge and release state callbacks such as ftDonkey_8010E7B4, ftDonkey_8010E840, ftDonkey_8010E8E0, ftDonkey_8010E930, ftDonkey_8010EB0C, ftDonkey_8010EC5C, ftDonkey_8010ECE8, ftDonkey_8010ED88, ftDonkey_8010EDD8, and ftDonkey_8010EF7C.

Implemented transition callbacks and physics handlers such as ftDonkey_8010F094, ftDonkey_8010F098, ftDonkey_8010F1D8, ftDonkey_8010F1DC, ftDonkey_8010F1E0, ftDonkey_8010F1E4, ftDonkey_8010F1E8, ftDonkey_8010F31C, ftDonkey_8010F320, ftDonkey_8010F324, ftDonkey_8010F328, ftDonkey_8010F348, ftDonkey_8010F368, ftDonkey_8010F388, ftDonkey_8010F3A8, ftDonkey_8010F3C8, ftDonkey_8010F3E8, ftDonkey_8010F408, ftDonkey_8010F428, ftDonkey_8010F448, ftDonkey_8010F468, ftDonkey_8010F50C, ftDonkey_8010F5B0, ftDonkey_8010F654, ftDonkey_8010F6F8, ftDonkey_8010F79C, ftDonkey_8010F840, ftDonkey_8010F8E4, ftDonkey_8010F988, and ftDonkey_8010FA2C.
Added effect cleanup helpers ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffects and ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffectsPlus, with the Plus variant also resetting fighter->sa.dk.x222C before calling efLib_DestroyAll.
Added SpecialHi callback setup and start actions in new ftdonkey6.c: ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialHi, ftDonkey_SpecialHi_StartAction, and ftDonkey_SpecialAirHi_StartAction.

SpecialHi start actions set action states 0x17D and 0x17E, reset ftcmd vars x2200 through x220C, consume jumps, call movement helpers func_8007CC78 or func_8007D440 using donkey_attr->x54, and spawn effect 0x4CA.
Expanded ftDonkeyAttributes with x48, x4C, x50, x54 and changed x30 to s32, matching its usage as an integer stride/count multiplier in SpecialN article or hitbox-index calculations.
Updated Donkey action-state table entries in the assembly file from anonymous func_/lbl_ labels to ftDonkey_8010... names, improving searchability and C linkage clarity.
smart_moves: Factored repeated callback assignments into ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialN and ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialHi instead of duplicating raw assignments in every state transition.
Factored callback cleanup into ftDonkey_NullCallbacks, making the repeated clearing of x21F0, x21D4, and x21D8 explicit and easier to match consistently.

Introduced ftDonkey_UpdateGroundVel for the repeated calculation fighter->xEC_ground_vel = facing_direction * donkey_attr->x34 * stateVar4, clarifying why x234C_stateVar4 needs a signed integer alias.
Converted common small-data float constants to direct 0.0f and 1.0f literals in ftdonkey5.c, which helped eliminate local sdata2 symbols from the remaining assembly.
Renamed table-referenced anonymous labels to ftDonkey_8010... symbols, preserving address-based names while moving toward subsystem-specific naming.

Used stateVar signed aliases such as x2348_stateVar3_s32, x234C_stateVar4_s32, x2350_stateVar5_s32, and x2354_stateVar6_s32 to express comparisons against -1 and integer arithmetic without unsafe casts.
Separated ftdonkey6.c into a new object and added it to obj_files.mk, allowing SpecialHi decompilation to proceed independently from the larger SpecialN-heavy ftdonkey5.c.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter special uses generic stateVar slots with mixed interpretations, add union aliases in Fighter rather than forcing every function to cast or use unsigned fields.
Attribute struct typing should follow actual usage: this PR changed ftDonkeyAttributes.x30 from f32 to s32 because the code multiplies it with an integer charge/state value for index-like offsets.
Small helper functions for callbacks and cleanup can reduce duplicated assembly patterns and make matching easier across ground and air variants.

Ground/air pairs often share most logic but differ in transition helpers: this PR used func_8008A2BC for grounded return and func_800CC730 or func_80096900 for aerial/end transitions where appropriate.
Effect spawning can reveal state-machine milestones: SpecialN waits for ftcmd var x2200, increments x2348_stateVar3, then spawns effect 0x4C8 on ground or 0x4C9 in air exactly once.
Preserving address-style ftDonkey_8010xxxx names is acceptable and useful when semantic names are not yet known, especially for functions referenced by action-state tables.
Adding a new C file requires both the source and an obj_files.mk entry; otherwise decompiled functions will not replace their assembly counterparts in the link.
naming_conventions: Use ftDonkey_8010XXXX for decompiled Donkey functions when the exact semantic name is not yet known.

Use subsystem-specific helper names for obvious repeated behavior, such as ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialN, ftDonkey_SetCallbacks_SpecialHi, ftDonkey_NullCallbacks, ftDonkey_UpdateGroundVel, ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffects, and ftDonkey_DestroyAllEffectsPlus.
Rename anonymous func_ or lbl_ action-table callbacks to ftDonkey_ address names once they are known to belong to Donkey.
Use signed stateVar aliases with _s32 suffix where the code compares against negative sentinels such as -1.
Use descriptive fighter callback field names already present in Fighter, such as x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage, x21E4_callback_OnDeath2, x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, and x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Direct 0.0f and 1.0f literals replaced lbl_804D95E0 and lbl_804D95E4 in ftdonkey5.c, allowing corresponding sdata2 constants to be deleted from the assembly file.
The integer-to-double conversion constant lbl_804D95D8 disappeared from this assembly slice after rewriting logic in C around typed integer fields and helper calculations.
For matching empty callbacks, the PR used explicit empty C functions such as ftDonkey_8010F094 and ftDonkey_8010F1D8 rather than leaving assembly stubs.
For transition callbacks that only call common physics helpers, the PR expressed them as thin wrappers around func_80084F3C or func_80084EEC.
Address-table renames in asm kept table data linked to newly compiled C functions without needing full semantic names.
The decompiled code mirrors animation-frame checks from assembly using ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining and fighter->x894_currentAnimFrame == 0.0f.
State machine sentinels such as x2350_stateVar5_s32 == -1 and x2354_stateVar6_s32 == -1 were represented explicitly to match assembly initialization and first-use behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to ftDonkey_8010E930, ftDonkey_8010EB0C, ftDonkey_8010EDD8, or ftDonkey_8010EF7C.
Check whether the typo-like local variable name 'unsued' in ftDonkey_8010ED88 was later cleaned up.
Look for subsequent Donkey PRs that finish moving remaining ftdonkey_2.s labels such as lbl_8010FD9C or lbl_8010FE60.
Search later struct updates for stronger names for ftDonkeyAttributes fields x30, x34, x38, x4C, and x54.
Compare this callback factoring pattern with other fighter special-move decompilations that set hitlag pause/resume callbacks.
